65d2b4d531bcc15f0a3cebba.question: In a work by this author, a bullfinch who “had fancied himself something of a despot” dies before it is revealed that Lady Anne “had been dead for two hours.” This author created a character who argues “beauty is only sin deep” while a woman “reputed to have read Maeterlinck’s Life of the Bee” tries to reform him. In a story by this author, Mavis Pellington is only invited to a party because she is considered “the only one. . . idiotic enough” to buy a car nicknamed (*) “The Envy of Sisyphus.” In a story by this author, a cat conveniently dies after revealing all of his owners’ secrets, having been taught to speak by Cornelius Appin. For 10 points, name this British short story writer who took his pen name from the Rubaïyat, the author of “Tobermory” and “The Open Window.”

For 10 points, name this British short story writer who took his pen name from the Rubaiyat, the author of "Tobermory" and "The Open Window."

65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebcd.question: Note to players: two answers required. One essay remarks that a collaboration between these two authors is strange due to its seemingly direct address of Europeans, whom that essay refers to as zombies. That essay is Judith Butler’s “Violence, Non-Violence.” In an essay, one of these authors refers to the other’s mistake as searching for the source of the spring, leading him to “drain the spring dry.” In a collaboration, one of these two authors wrote of a golden age where people from Paris, London, and Amsterdam called “Parthenon! Brotherhood!” and were echoed elsewhere “…thenon! …therhood!” A remark that “The Jewishness of the Jew can go unnoticed” appears in an essay by one of these authors that finds a flaw in the other’s conception of race as put forth in the essay (*) “Anti-Semite and Jew.” Tsitsi Dangaremba’s novel Nervous Conditions takes its title from a preface one of these authors wrote for a book by the other. One of these authors critiques the other’s essay “Black Orpheus” in “The Lived Experience of the Black Man,” an essay in his collection Black Skin, White Masks. For 10 points, name these two authors, one of whom wrote the introduction to the other’s The Wretched of the Earth and the play Dirty Hands.

A remark that "The Jewishness of the Jew can go unnoticed" appears in an essay by one of these authors that finds a flaw in the other's conception of race as put forth in the essay (*) "Anti-Semite and Jew." Tsitsi Dangaremba's novel Nervous Conditions takes its title from a preface one of these authors wrote for a book by the other. One of these authors critiques the other's essay "Black Orpheus" in "The Lived Experience of the Black Man," an essay in his collection Black Skin, White Masks. 65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd0.question: Bad militaristic parodies of a poem by this author, including one ending with the line "Warsaw also falls," are translated as similarly bad militaristic parodies of "I Wandered Lonely As A Cloud" in Bridgman and Timms' version of Kraus' The Last Days of Mankind. A quote from this author is followed by the line "It is the writer's grief. It is the wild / March wind" in John Shade's "Pale Fire." A poem by this author notes that its subject loved "the spring, the summer, the autumn," and doesn't have to worry about winter, because he's dead. This author noted how (*) "The little birds were silent in the wood" in a conspicuously long line of a very short poem that begins with the claim that "Over all hilltops / is peace." This author of "Anacreon's Grave" and two "Wanderer's Night Songs" wrote of a creature who promises, "My daughters shall lead the nightly dances / And rock and dance and sing you to sleep" to a child on a horse, who ends up dying in his father's arms. For 10 points, the line "Who rides so late in the night and the wind?" opens what poet's "Der Erlkönig"?
65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd0.question_sanitized: Bad militaristic parodies of a poem by this author, including one ending with the line "Warsaw also falls," are translated as similarly bad militaristic parodies of "I Wandered Lonely As A Cloud" in Bridgman and Timms' version of Kraus' The Last Days of Mankind. A quote from this author is followed by the line "It is the writer's grief. It is the wild / March wind" in John Shade's "Pale Fire." A poem by this author notes that its subject loved "the spring, the summer, the autumn," and doesn't have to worry about winter, because he's dead. This author noted how (*) "The little birds were silent in the wood" in a conspicuously long line of a very short poem that begins with the claim that "Over all hilltops / is peace." This author of "Anacreon's Grave" and two "Wanderer's Night Songs" wrote of a creature who promises, "My daughters shall lead the nightly dances / And rock and dance and sing you to sleep" to a child on a horse, who ends up dying in his father's arms. For 10 points, the line "Who rides so late in the night and the wind?" opens what poet's "Der Erlkonig"?
65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd0.answer: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe (The "Warsaw also falls" parody is called "Wandrers Schlachtlied," and is absolutely fucking incredible. I provide it here: "Also über allen Gipfeln ist Ruh' / Über allen Wipfeln spürest du / Kaum einen Hauch. / Der Hindenburg schlafet im Walde, / Warte nur balde / Fällt Warschau auch." There's also a perhaps even funnier parody about a baker. Shade quotes the first line of "Der Erlkönig" in "Pale Fire.")
65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd0.answer_sanitized: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe (The "Warsaw also falls" parody is called "Wandrers Schlachtlied," and is absolutely fucking incredible. I provide it here: "Also uber allen Gipfeln ist Ruh' / Uber allen Wipfeln spurest du / Kaum einen Hauch. / Der Hindenburg schlafet im Walde, / Warte nur balde / Fallt Warschau auch." There's also a perhaps even funnier parody about a baker. Shade quotes the first line of "Der Erlkonig" in "Pale Fire.")

65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd4.question: The former right-wing Croatian mercenary and French spy Francis Mirkovic narrates a one-sentence novel set in one of these places, Mathias Enard's Zone. In a novel set mostly in one of these places, a man connects the images in Panini's Modern Rome to places he visited with his lover while on trips for the firm Scabelli. While in one of these places, a typewriter salesman decides not to leave his wife Henriette for his mistress Cecile in a second-person novel by Michel Butor, La Modification. The dancer (*) Coral Musker and the businessman Carleton Myatt begin an affair in one of these places in a Graham Greene novel. From one of these places, the speaker of a poem sees "nylon gloves and jewellery-substitutes, / The lemons, mauves and olive-ochres." At the end of a poem set in one of these places, the speaker describes "a sense of falling, like an arrow-shower / Sent out of sight, somewhere becoming rain"; that poem is set at "about / One-twenty on the sunlit Saturday." For 10 points, name these places, one of which is the setting of Larkin's "The Whitsun Weddings."

65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd9.question: At the end of a story by this author, one character is told that "but for thine indiscretion thou might have had two" instants with a frequently-disappearing maiden, whose name is "Happiness." The title character of that story worships Hastur. In another story by this author, which claims to have been "imparted to the medium Bayrolles by the spirit Hoseib Alar Robardin," a sick man transported to a desolate landscape learns that he is wandering in the ruins of the city in which he once lived. This author included "Haita the Shepherd" and (*) "An Inhabitant of Carcosa" in the proto-weird collection Can Such Things Be?, which also includes a story about the death of Hugh Morgan. One of this author's characters muses on the dictum, "Consciousness is the Creature of Rhythm" while walking home from a conversation in which he had asked, "Do you really believe that a machine thinks?" A story by this author ends with the narrator being saved from a house fire by the machinist Haley. For 10 points, name this author, who wrote of a robot who murders its inventor after losing at chess in "Moxon's Master."

65d2b4d631bcc15f0a3cebd9.answer: Ambrose Bierce [or Ambrose Gwinnett Bierce] (The one unnamed story is "The Damned Thing.")

The covers of English translations of Rainald Goetz' novels Insane and Rave are this color. 65d2b4d831bcc15f0a3cec3f.question: In one story by this author, one character taboos words such as “ringworm”, “lamp” and “candle” and flies into a rage whenever accidentally using them. In another work by this author, the narrator hears of a man’s heart and liver being eaten after being beaten to death in the Wolf Cub village and ends (*) “Save the children!” In one story by this author, the title character loses his pigtail after it is cut off by a “Foreign Imitation Devil.” That character created by this author slaps himself after losing fights to justify them as “spiritual victories.”For 10 points, name this Chinese author of A Madman’s Diary and The True Story of Ah Q.
65d2b4d831bcc15f0a3cec3f.question_sanitized: In one story by this author, one character taboos words such as "ringworm", "lamp" and "candle" and flies into a rage whenever accidentally using them. In another work by this author, the narrator hears of a man's heart and liver being eaten after being beaten to death in the Wolf Cub village and ends (*) "Save the children!" In one story by this author, the title character loses his pigtail after it is cut off by a "Foreign Imitation Devil." That character created by this author slaps himself after losing fights to justify them as "spiritual victories."For 10 points, name this Chinese author of A Madman's Diary and The True Story of Ah Q.

65d2b4d831bcc15f0a3cec41.question: In one poem from this country, the narrator asks “why you did not or could not give answer when Langston Hughes who wondered as he wandered asked what happens to a dream deferred” and begins “An omelette cannot be unscrambled”. That Poet Laureate of “No Serenity Here” from this country was rapper Earl Sweatshirt’s father. In a novel by another author from this country, the stray Katy is put down at (*) Bev Shaw’s dog pound after living at a farm co-owned by Petrus. An affair with the student Melanie Isaacs causes the protagonist to move to his daughter Lucy’s farm. For 10 points, name this home country of Disgraced author J.M Coetzee.
